rulings.Case Title: CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BURL STEVEN WALKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Issuance For Osc Why Additional Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed For Failure To Comply With Court Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 21, 2011, line PLAINTIFF CHICAGO TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY Application For Issuance For Osc Why Additional Sanctions Should Not Be Imposed For Failure To Comply With Court Order The Application for Issuance of an OSC is denied. The court did not receive courtesy copies of the moving papers. This motion was continued from 9/27 for the moving party to provide courtesy copies of moving papers, but those papers have still not been received. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS WEISEL VS. RYAN ASSOCIATES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 21, 2011, line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT DORVIN D. LEIS CO., INC. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS - Granted. Cross-complainant Ryan Associates has not met its burden of establishing either sufficient contacts of Dorvin D. Leis Co. with California or a forum selection clause binding that company. The purchase of construction products by a Hawaii company from a California company for use in a Hawaiian construction project for plaintiff is insufficient to establish specific jurisdiction. Nor is the purchase of construction products by a Hawaiian company from California companies for use in Hawaiian projects on an ongoing basis sufficient to establish general jurisdiction. The forum selection clause in the owner/general contractor contract does not become applicable to any disputes with Dorvin D. Leis Co. merely by the language that ¿Subcontractor ¿ will abide by the¿ General Contract. There is nothing in the language of the forum selection clause in the General Contract that makes it applicable to a subcontractor. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. JACKSON & WALLACE LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 21, 2011, line 3, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Denied. The Bank failed to sustain its burden of production on the amount owed by Ms. Jackson. The Bank did not present any admissible evidence on this issue. Defendant¿s objection to the first sentence of the Butzbaugh declaration and exhibit 6 thereto are sustained. Plaintiff fails to show that exhibit 6 meets the business records hearsay exception of Evidence Code § 1271. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
